name: Whole Wheat-Rye Bread
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 0:30
1. Dissolve yeast in the warm water using a large mixing bowl; let stand for 5 minutes.
2. Stir in molasses, salt, caraway seeds, shortening, 1-3/4 cups of lukewarm water, rye flour, and whole wheat flour.
3. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until the dough is smooth.
4. Gradually stir in enough all-purpose flour to make a soft dough.
5. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface, and knead for 8 to 10 minutes until it's smooth and elastic.
6. Place the dough in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease the top.
7. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free from drafts, for 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
8. Punch down the dough; roll into a rectangle measuring 19 x 16 inches.
9. Roll the dough jellyroll fashion, starting at the long side, and pinch the seam and ends to seal.
10. Place the loaf, seam side down, on a greased baking sheet.
11. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, for 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
12. Brush the loaf with egg white.
13. Bake at 400°F for 35 to 40 minutes or until the loaf sounds hollow when tapped.
14. Let cool on a wire rack.
Yield: 1 loaf
